title: pyrus nivalis var. austriaca
intro: Pyrus nivalis var. austriaca, also known as the Austrian mountain pear or mountain pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, the bark and leaves of Pyrus nivalis var. austriaca have been used in herbal medicine due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and acting as an antiseptic agent to prevent infections. The bark and leaves of Pyrus nivalis var. austriaca can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions.
